zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Bello Monte於大約1200年出生在英國英格蘭戴文維爾時，他的父親Sir Phillip de Bello Monte18歲，母親Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte21歲。他和Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de Bello Monte于大约1200年出生在英国英格兰戴文維爾时，他的父亲Sir Phillip de Bello Monte18岁，母亲Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte21岁。他和Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bello
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Jorge, Mario, Roberto, Eduardo, Sergio, Alberto, Ernesto, Julio.  Portuguese Joaquim, Paulo.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Natale, Vito, Eliseo, Luciano, Sal, Aldo, Amato.

Spanish and Italian: from bello ‘handsome’ (from Late Latin bellus), hence a nickname for a handsome man or perhaps in some instances ironically for an ugly one. In medieval Italy the word was also applied as an omen or well-wishing name.
Catalan (Belló): shortened form of Abelló (see Abello ).

variants.name: Belloli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Angelo, Marcello, Stefano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of   Bello  .

variants.name: Belle
meanings: Italian (Bellé): from a shortened derivative of Bello , for example Belletto.
French: from the feminine form of the nickname Bel , from Old French belle ‘beautiful’, or a habitational name from (La) Belle, the name of several places in various parts of France.
German: habitational name from a place so named in the Lippe district.

variants.name: Abello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Gustavo, Alberto, Bernardo, Carols, Edgardo, Eduardo, Fortunato, Guillermo.
Catalan (Abelló): nickname from the masculine form of abella ‘bee’ (see Abella ).
Asturian-Leonese: topographic name from a Castilianized form of abeyu ‘shelter’. Compare Avello .

variants.name: Belli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Aldo, Remo, Caesar, Dino, Guido, Antonio, Edo, Elio, Enrico, Ferdinando, Guilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Bello  .

variants.name: Bellido
meanings: Spanish: from a medieval nickname or personal name Bellitus, a variant of Bello .

variants.name: Bellot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Benito, Manuel, Alejandra, Felipe, Jovita, Juan, Maricela, Pedro.
Catalan: from a diminutive of Belló (see Bello ).
French: nickname from a derivative of   Bel   ‘beautiful’.

variants.name: Bellone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Salvatore, Stefano, Vincenza.  French Baptiste, Camille.
Italian: from an augmentative of   Bello  . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Bellino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Vito, Salvatore, Carmine, Aldo, Antonio, Constantino, Dino, Domenic, Donato, Gaetano, Geno.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Bello  .

variants.name: Bella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Romeo, Salvatore, Aldo, Alfonso, Alfredo, Antonio, Berto, Carlo, Dario, Italia, Napoli, Sebastiano, Vita.  Spanish Miguel, Carmelita, Eleuterio, Elida, Graciela, Juan, Lazaro, Maria De La Luz.
Italian and Spanish: from the female personal name Bella, meaning ‘beautiful, lovely’ (see Bello ). Compare Di Bella .
Italian: possibly also a habitational name from Basilicata Bella, in Potenza, probably so named with Latin labellum ‘little basin’.

variants.name: Bellante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Carmelo, Santo.
Italian:
from a derivative of the nickname   Bello  .

nameMeanings.name: Monte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Mario, Domenic, Salvatore, Aldo, Angelo, Antonio, Arturo, Carmelo, Gaspare, Carmela, Ciro, Gasper, Philomena.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Edgardo, Emilia.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese monte (from Latin mons, genitive montis).
Italian and Jewish (from Italy): habitational name from any of the places called with Monte or Monti. Compare   Del Monte  .

variants.name: Monti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Aniello, Pasquale, Aldo, Carmel, Elio, Enrico, Gaetano, Marco, Marino, Philomena.
Italian: variant of   Monte  , from the plural.

variants.name: Montagna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Sal, Gaetano, Alessandro, Antonino, Attilio, Carlo, Carmelo, Cosmo, Dino, Enrico, Oreste.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain or mountains, Italian montagna, from Late Latin montanea, originally the neuter plural of an adjective derivative of mons (see Monte 1), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word.

variants.name: Montini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Enio, Pietro, Arsenio, Concetta, Dante, Dino, Domenic, Eliseo, Lorenzo, Marco, Mauro, Quirino, Ricco, Tulio.
Italian (Milan):
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Montes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Jorge, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Carlos, Alfredo, Ramon, Raul.  Portuguese Joaquim.

Spanish (also Montés); Portuguese (also Montês): topographic name for a mountain dweller, from the plural form of Monte ‘mountain’ or from the adjective Spanish montés, Portuguese montês ‘of the mountain’.

variants.name: Montello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Biagio, Carmela, Marco.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Del Monte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Angelo, Rocco, Giovanni, Pompeo, Antonio, Armelio, Berta, Camillo, Carmela, Deno, Elvio, Ezio, Gilda, Giulio.  Spanish Angel, Gonzalo, Guillermina, Jesus, Jose, Jose Manuel.
Italian, Spanish, and Jewish (from Italy): variant, with the preposition and definite article del ‘from the’, of Monte .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Montejo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Raul, Carlos, Eduardo, Abelardo, Alberto, Julio, Mario, Mauricio, Miguel, Miguel Angel, Alejandro, Alfonso.
Spanish:
topographic name from a diminutive of   Monte   1.

variants.name: Montesi
meanings: Italian:
habitational name for someone from a place called   Monte  , from an adjectival form of the placename.
from the plural of Montese, a habitational name from Montese in Modena province.

variants.name: Montas
meanings: Spanish and Catalan (Montás); French: derivative of Spanish monte, Catalan and Old French mont ‘mountain’ (see Monte , Mont ). This surname is most common in the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Bello Monte was born about 1200, in Shirwell, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Phillip de Bello Monte, was 18 and his mother, Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, was 21. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Bello Monte est né environ 1200, à Shirwell, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Phillip de Bello Monte, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Bello Monte um 1200 in Shirwell, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Phillip de Bello Monte 18 und seine Mutter Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte 21. Er hatte mit Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Bello Monte è nato il circa 1200 nel Shirwell, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Phillip de Bello Monte, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Bello Monteが約1200年にイギリスイギリスデボンシュイルウエルで生まれた時，父Sir Phillip de Bello Monteは18歳，母Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monteは21歳であった。彼はMrs. Richard de Bello Monteとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Bello Monte(이)가 대략 1200년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 실웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Phillip de Bello Monte(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Bello Monte nasceu aproximadamente 1200, em Shirwell, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Phillip de Bello Monte, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Bello Monte родился около 1200 г. в местности ширвелл, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Phillip de Bello Monte было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard de Bello Monte nació aproximadamente 1200, en Shirwell, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Phillip de Bello Monte, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Mrs. Phillip de Bello Monte, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Richard de Bello Monte.
